title: Low DO and filamentous growth risk
core_statement: Persistently low dissolved oxygen may favor the proliferation of some filamentous organisms and increase settling-related risk.
optional_notes: Use this statement conservatively and verify against SVI, SV30, and microscopy observations.

title: Paramecium-like free swimmers can accompany low DO concern
core_statement: In the internal spreadsheet reference, 侧滴虫 is listed as appearing together with low dissolved oxygen and sludge disintegration concern.
optional_notes: Treat this as a retrieval cue rather than a confirmed diagnosis rule. Verify with aeration data and standardized microscopy review.

title: Bean-shaped protozoa note for low DO context
core_statement: In the internal spreadsheet reference, 豆形虫 is associated with low dissolved oxygen and sludge disintegration concern.
optional_notes: Use as a conservative association only. Do not infer the plant-wide mechanism from this organism alone.

title: Wudichong note for low DO context
core_statement: In the internal spreadsheet reference, 屋滴虫 is associated with low dissolved oxygen and sludge disintegration concern.
optional_notes: This card should support retrieval only. Cross-check with dissolved oxygen trend, floc condition, and settling indicators.

title: Duobochong note for severe purification deterioration
trigger_cues: 污泥净化性能严重恶化
core_statement: In the internal spreadsheet reference, 多波虫 is listed together with severe deterioration of sludge purification performance.
optional_notes: Use only as an alerting cue. Confirm with effluent quality data and broader microscopy observations before proposing action.
